Request a Demo

How It Installs

Under 60 minutes per asset.
At the MCC, not on the machine.

CT/VT clamps at the motor control cabinet, a SAM4 DAQ device, and a power supply. No sensors on the machine, no scaffolding, no ATEX permits. Your electrical maintenance team handles the full install.

How SAM4 connects

The installation explained in 5 seconds

Motor cable Existing power supply
→
CT/VT clamps Clamp-on current and voltage
→
SAM4 DAQ DIN-rail mounted in the MCC
→
Ethernet + Cellular PoE between DAQs, 4G/LTE upstream
→
SAM4 Cloud AI monitoring live

Why the MCC

Traditional monitoring needs sensors bolted to the machine. ESA doesn't.

30–40% of critical rotating equipment sits beyond the reach of conventional monitoring: submerged pumps, sealed blowers, hazardous zones, remote sites. SAM4 starts exactly here, with the assets that keep reliability engineers awake at night.

Inaccessible assets

Submerged pumps, sealed compressors, and confined-space equipment where vibration sensors can't be installed or maintained.

Hazardous zones

ATEX and Ex-rated environments where installing and maintaining wired sensors requires costly shutdowns and permits.

Wiring complexity

Assets hundreds of metres from the nearest cabinet. Traditional monitoring means cable runs, conduit, and weeks of integration work.

SAM4 installs at the motor control cabinet in under 60 minutes. No wiring to the machine. No sensors on the asset. See how it installs →

Installation process

Five steps. One shift. First data within hours.

Every installation follows the same process, whether you're connecting 5 assets in a pilot or 1,000 across a fleet. Your electrical maintenance team runs the install.

01

Preparation and MCC isolation

De-energize the relevant MCC section per your site's lockout/tagout procedure. Verify isolation before opening the cabinet. The motor is temporarily stopped during CT/VT installation only.

Standard LOTO procedure

02

CT/VT clamp installation

Current transformers clamp onto each phase of the motor supply cable. Voltage measurement connects via fused terminal blocks. Phase order matters: match CT orientation to cable labelling.

Per motor feeder

03

DAQ device setup

The SAM4 DAQ mounts on a DIN rail inside or adjacent to the MCC. Connect CT/VT leads to the DAQ. Multiple DAQ units chain together via Power over Ethernet (PoE). One power supply unit feeds the chain.

One DAQ per motor feeder

04

Connectivity and verification

A modem kit provides the cellular (4G/LTE) uplink. No corporate network needed. After re-energizing, verify LED status on each DAQ, then commission via the SAM4.local browser interface.

No IT tickets, no firewall changes

05

Cloud activation

Once the DAQ is online, Samotics configures the asset in SAM4. Baseline data collection begins immediately. AI models start learning the motor's normal signature within hours.

First diagnostics within 48 hours

<60 min

Typical installation time per asset

Your existing electrical maintenance team. Standard tools. No specialist contractors.

Photo CT clamps on motor cable Close-up of CT/VT clamps installed on three-phase cable inside MCC
Photo SAM4 DAQ on DIN rail DAQ device mounted on DIN rail inside motor control cabinet
Photo Completed MCC installation Full view of MCC with SAM4 hardware installed and cabinet closed

What this eliminates

Everything that makes traditional monitoring slow, expensive, or impossible

Every item below is a real deployment barrier that has killed or delayed condition monitoring projects. SAM4 removes all of them.

Installation barriers

No sensors on machinesNo mounting brackets, adhesive pads, or cable runs to the asset.

No ATEX permitsThe MCC is in the safe zone. No hot work permits, no gas testing.

Infrastructure barriers

No network changesCellular connectivity bypasses IT infrastructure entirely.

No scaffolding or accessNo working at height. The MCC is always accessible.

Operational barriers

Minimal production impactThe MCC section is de-energized briefly for CT/VT installation, then re-energized. The motor returns to service the same shift.

No specialist contractorYour qualified electrical maintenance team installs. No external coordination.

Deployment scale

Same process at every scale

The installation process doesn't change between a 5-asset pilot and a 1,000-asset fleet rollout. Only the number of shifts changes.

1
10–50 assets

Pilot

Prove the technology on critical assets. 1–2 days. First diagnostics within 2 weeks.

2
100–500 assets

Site

Full site coverage. Work through MCCs systematically. 1–2 weeks. Zero disruption.

3
1,000+ assets

Fleet

Multi-site rollout. Same hardware, same process. Fleet management from day one.

See deployment results →

"The major reason we chose SAM4 is because it's easy to install in the motor control center, far away from our ATEX-zoned production systems. That greatly reduces the cost of equipment, installation and cabling, compared to traditional condition monitoring techniques."

Joost Cuyckens Asset Management Leader, Kuraray

See exactly what's involved. We'll scope your MCC, asset list, and timeline.

Start your asset audit Schedule a technical deep-dive

Monitoring the assets your sensors can't reach.
